1.texto_original: Si te has de convertir, oh Israel, dice Jehová, conviértete a mí; y si quitares de delante de mí tus abominaciones, no andarás de acá para allá.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 4:1

"Si te has de convertir, oh Israel, dice Jehová, conviértete a mí; y si quitares de delante de mí tus abominaciones, no andarás de acá para allá."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremías 4:1

Philological Analysis

"Si te has de convertir, oh Israel, dice Jehová, conviértete a mí; y si quitares de delante de mí tus abominaciones, no andarás de acá para allá."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Si te has de convertir, oh Israel, dice Jehová, conviértete a mí; y si quitares de delante de mí tus abominaciones, no andarás de acá para allá."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Porque una voz proclama desde Dan, y anuncia calamidad desde el monte de Efraín.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 4:15

"Porque una voz proclama desde Dan, y anuncia calamidad desde el monte de Efraín."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremías 4:15

Philological Analysis

"Porque una voz proclama desde Dan, y anuncia calamidad desde el monte de Efraín."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

29.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Por el estruendo de la gente de a caballo y de los arqueros huirá toda la ciudad; entrarán en las espesuras de los bosques y subirán a los peñascos; todas las ciudades serán abandonadas, y no quedará en ellas morador alguno."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

30.texto_original: Y tú, desolada, ¿qué harás? Aunque te vistas de grana, aunque te adornes con atavíos de oro, aunque pintes con antimonio tus ojos, en vano te engalanas; te menospreciaron tus amantes, buscarán tu vida.
30.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 4:30

"Y tú, desolada, ¿qué harás? Aunque te vistas de grana, aunque te adornes con atavíos de oro, aunque pintes con antimonio tus ojos, en vano te engalanas; te menospreciaron tus amantes, buscarán tu vida."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

31.texto_original: Porque oí una voz como de mujer que está de parto, angustia como de primeriza; voz de la hija de Sión que lamenta y extiende sus manos, [diciendo]: ¡Ay ahora de mí! que mi alma desmaya a causa de los asesinos.
